nodes.name: detect_signal_onsets_elgendi2013
nodes.description: Detects physiological signal onsets (e.g., ECG R-peaks) using the Elgendi et al. (2013) method. This algorithm typically involves bandpass filtering, squaring, generating potential blocks of interest, and applying dynamic thresholding to identify definitive peaks.

inputs.name: signal
inputs.constraints: 1D numerical array representing the physiological signal.

inputs.name: sampling_rate
inputs.constraints: The sampling frequency of the signal in Hz.

inputs.name: peakwindow
inputs.constraints: Duration of the peak detection window in seconds, used for identifying blocks of interest.

inputs.name: beatwindow
inputs.constraints: Duration of the beat classification window in seconds, used for dynamic thresholding.

inputs.name: beatoffset
inputs.constraints: Duration of the offset for the beat classification window in seconds.

inputs.name: mindelay
inputs.constraints: Minimum delay between consecutive detected onsets in seconds.

outputs.name: onset_indices
outputs.constraints: An array of integer indices corresponding to the detected onsets within the input signal.

nodes.type_signature: (signal: np.ndarray, sampling_rate: float, peakwindow: float, beatwindow: float, beatoffset: float, mindelay: float) -> np.ndarray
nodes.conceptual_summary: adaptive_threshold_feature_detection. Identifies significant, localized events within a 1D numerical series. The transform first isolates a target frequency band, then amplifies potential event regions by applying a non-linear function (squaring). It subsequently uses a moving-window statistical measure to dynamically set thresholds, discerning true event points from background noise.. Applications: Detecting the onset of P-waves or S-waves in a seismogram to locate an earthquake's epicenter., Identifying transient stellar flares or exoplanet transits in an astronomical light curve from a telescope., Pinpointing the exact moment of micro-fractures in a material by analyzing high-frequency acoustic emission data during stress testing., Detecting anomalous volume spikes in high-frequency financial trading data to flag market events.

nodes.name: preprocess_signal
nodes.description: Creates a copy of the input signal, truncates any values below zero to rectify it, and computes the square of the result. This enhances features for peak detection.

inputs.name: signal
inputs.constraints: 1D array of physiological data.

outputs.name: processed_signal
outputs.constraints: Rectified (non-negative) version of the input signal.

outputs.name: squared_signal
outputs.constraints: The element-wise square of the processed signal.

nodes.name: compute_signal_envelopes
nodes.description: Calculates two moving average filters over the squared signal using different window sizes (peakwindow and beatwindow) to create distinct signal envelopes for thresholding and block identification.

inputs.name: squared_signal
inputs.constraints: A non-negative, squared signal.

inputs.name: sampling_rate
inputs.constraints: The signal's sampling rate in Hz.

inputs.name: peakwindow
inputs.constraints: The time window in seconds for the peak-detection moving average.

inputs.name: beatwindow
inputs.constraints: The time window in seconds for the beat-detection moving average.

outputs.name: peak_envelope
outputs.constraints: Smoothed envelope for identifying potential peak regions.

outputs.name: beat_envelope
outputs.constraints: Smoothed envelope for establishing a dynamic beat threshold.

nodes.type_signature: (squared_signal: np.ndarray, sampling_rate: float, peakwindow: float, beatwindow: float) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]

nodes.name: calculate_dynamic_threshold
nodes.description: Computes a dynamic threshold by combining the beat envelope with a scaled mean of the squared signal's energy. This allows the threshold to adapt to local signal variations.

inputs.name: beat_envelope
inputs.constraints: The beat detection envelope.

inputs.name: squared_signal
inputs.constraints: The squared signal, used to calculate mean energy.

inputs.name: beatoffset
inputs.constraints: A scaling factor to adjust the threshold's sensitivity.

outputs.name: threshold
outputs.constraints: An adaptive threshold array of the same length as the signal.

nodes.type_signature: (beat_envelope: np.ndarray, squared_signal: np.ndarray, beatoffset: float) -> np.ndarray

nodes.name: identify_candidate_blocks
nodes.description: Identifies continuous blocks of interest ('waves') where the peak envelope exceeds the dynamic threshold. These blocks are candidates for containing signal onsets.

inputs.name: peak_envelope
inputs.constraints: The peak detection envelope.

inputs.name: threshold
inputs.constraints: The dynamic threshold for comparison.

outputs.name: candidate_blocks
outputs.constraints: A list of (start_index, end_index) tuples for each candidate block.

nodes.name: extract_prominent_peaks_from_blocks
nodes.description: Filters candidate blocks by a minimum length criterion and then, within each valid block, finds the single most prominent peak in the processed signal.

inputs.name: processed_signal
inputs.constraints: The rectified signal from which to find peaks.

inputs.name: candidate_blocks
inputs.constraints: List of (start, end) indices for blocks to search within.

inputs.name: sampling_rate
inputs.constraints: The signal's sampling rate in Hz.

inputs.name: peakwindow
inputs.constraints: Used to calculate the minimum required length of a block.

outputs.name: prominent_peaks
outputs.constraints: A list of indices corresponding to the most prominent peaks found in the valid blocks.

nodes.type_signature: (processed_signal: np.ndarray, candidate_blocks: list[tuple[int, int]], sampling_rate: float, peakwindow: float) -> list[int]

nodes.name: filter_peaks_by_minimum_delay
nodes.description: Filters the list of prominent peaks to ensure that the time delay between any two consecutive peaks is above a minimum threshold. This removes falsely detected adjacent peaks.

inputs.name: prominent_peaks
inputs.constraints: A list of peak indices.

inputs.name: sampling_rate
inputs.constraints: The signal's sampling rate in Hz.

inputs.name: mindelay
inputs.constraints: The minimum required delay in seconds between consecutive peaks.

outputs.name: onset_indices
outputs.constraints: A 1D array of the final, filtered onset indices.

nodes.type_signature: (prominent_peaks: list[int], sampling_rate: float, mindelay: float) -> np.ndarray
